records.ety: [OE. galwes, pl., AS. galga, gealga, gallows, cross; akin to D. galg gallows, OS. & OHG. galgo, G. galgen, Icel. gālgi, Sw. & Dan. galge, Goth. galga a cross. Etymologically and historically considered, gallows is a noun in the plural number, but it is used as a singular, and hence is preceded by a; as, a gallows.]

defs: A frame from which is suspended the rope with which criminals are executed by hanging, usually consisting of two upright posts and a crossbeam on the top; also, a like frame for suspending anything.
quotes: So they hanged Haman on the gallows.
If I hang, I'll make a fat pair of gallows.
O, there were desolation of gaolers and gallowses!

defs: A wretch who deserves the gallows.
The rest for the tympan when raised.
A pair of suspenders or braces.
quotes: At length him naild on a gallow tree.
